crime.value: Mysterious disappearance of Neville St. Clair from opium den; question: what happened to him and where is he now?
crime.quote: what Neville St. Clair was doing in the opium den, what happened to him when there, where is he now

victim.value: Neville St. Clair, apparently respectable man with secret connection to opium den, making him vulnerable
victim.quote: what Neville St. Clair was doing in the opium den

closed_world.value: The opium den and immediate vicinity where St. Clair was last seen, with known occupants including Hugh Boone
closed_world.quote: what Hugh Boone had to do with his disappearance

culprit_and_motive.value: Sir George Burnwell and Mary (niece), motivated by greed for gold coronet and romantic manipulation
culprit_and_motive.quote: His wicked lust for gold kindled at the news, and he bent her to his will

resolution.value: Holmes deduces the truth through logical analysis and confronts the culprits with evidence of their scheme
resolution.quote: When I had cleared it all up I had an interview with him, and finding that he would not tell me the story

investigator.value: Sherlock Holmes as official consulting detective, with Dr. Watson as narrator and companion
investigator.quote: While Sherlock Holmes had been detailing this singular series of events

suspect_architecture.value: Multiple suspects across cases: Irene Adler (blackmailer), Hugh Boone/Neville St. Clair (identity mystery), Sir George Burnwell and Mary (conspiracy theft)
suspect_architecture.quote: Sir George Burnwell and your niece Mary. They have now fled together.

red_herring.value: Hugh Boone as separate beggar when he is actually the missing Neville St. Clair in disguise
red_herring.quote: He had for years been known as a professional beggar, but his life appeared to have been a very quiet

clue_fairness.value: Holmes shares his deductive process and evidence - footprints in snow, window access, character revelations
clue_fairness.quote: His footmarks had pressed right through the snow, so long had he stood there.

social_world.value: Victorian class structures - banking family, professional beggars, dubious characters, marriage expectations and family loyalty
social_world.quote: He is one of the most dangerous men in England--a ruined gambler, an absolutely desperate villain

alibi.value: Mary's apparent innocence contradicted by secret meetings with Burnwell through the stable lane window
alibi.quote: slipped down and talked to her lover through the window which leads into the stable lane

reveal_mechanic.value: Holmes reconstructs the crime through physical evidence and psychological deduction, then confronts the suspects
reveal_mechanic.quote: I will show you the steps by which I reached it

investigator_wound.value: Holmes's emotional detachment challenged by Irene Adler, the one woman who affected his rational mind
investigator_wound.quote: And yet there was but one woman to him, and that woman was the late Irene Adler

_meta.title: ebook sir arthur conan doyle the adventures of sherlock holmes
